data.title: Top 10 AIF performance for the month of April 2025
data.description:
Top 10 AIF performance for the month of April 2025

The top-performing Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) for the month of April saw CCV Investment Managers LLP’s Emerging Opportunities Fund-I leading the pack, at 8.61% returns for the month. Aarth AIF’s Growth Fund and Finideas Growth Fund Scheme-1 followed with returns of 7.86% and 7.36% respectively.

Top 10 AIF Performers for the month of April

Note: The historical returns timeline in each chart is different because of the variation in inception timelines of all funds. The top 10 data is based on calculations uptill April 30.

CCV Investment Managers LLP: Emerging Opportunities Fund-I: The fund has a sector-agnostic approach but primarily focuses on investing in small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) across India. It actively participates as an anchor investor in SME IPOs (invested in 8 such IPOs so far). Managed by Sahil Garg, the fund follows a bottom-up strategy.

Aarth AIF Growth Fund: Managed by Rishi Agarwal, the fund targets superior risk-adjusted returns by investing in listed equities, late-stage unlisted companies, IPOs, QIB placements, and private placements, with up to 40% of assets in unlisted securities and a 10% cap per company.

Finideas Growth Fund Scheme-1: It deploys systematic strategies across index, stock, and currency derivatives, while also hedging naked positions when necessary. The fund actively invests in Indian listed derivatives such as Nifty and Bank Nifty options and futures, stock futures and options, as well as other approved equity derivative products. In addition, it can allocate capital to equity shares, debt securities, ETFs, and other SEBI-permitted instruments.

Alchemy Capital Management: Leaders of Tomorrow - 2

Managed by Hiren Ved, the fund required minimum of 65% allocation to mid and small-cap stocks alongside select large-cap opportunities. The fund also targets around 10% allocation to IPOs or other instruments permitted under applicable laws.

Negen Undiscovered Value Fund: Managed by Jigar Dinesh Shah, the Fund focuses on overlooked mid-cap and small-cap stocks. Its approach emphasizes sectoral weightage in industrials (25%) and materials (20%), targeting undervalued assets with catalysts for re-rating.

Guardian Capital Partners Opportunities Scheme: Managed by Manav Saraf, the fund’s strategy is to buy fundamentally strong companies only when they are trading at significant discounts to their long-term average valuations, with a particular emphasis on large caps. Otherwise, the fund remains in cash rather than compromising on valuation discipline. Exits are made once stocks move above their historical average valuations, without waiting for peak prices.

StepTrade Revolution Fund: Managed by Kresha Gupta, the Revolution Fund focuses on mid-cap growth stocks with a momentum-based strategy, emphasizing sectoral weightage in healthcare (30%) and industrials (20%).

360 ONE Turnaround Opportunities Fund: Managed by Mehul Jani, the fund the fund invests in equities and related securities of companies especially those where short-term cycles have temporarily impacted profitability and growth.

Edelweiss Asset Management Consumer Trends Fund: Managed by Nalin Moni, Edelweiss Consumption Fund invests 99.97% of its assets in domestic equities, with a portfolio allocation of 54.84% to large-cap stocks, 15.07% to mid-cap stocks, and 5.97% to small-cap stocks. This sectoral/thematic fund is suitable for investors who are willing to take higher-risk bets for the potential of higher returns compared to broader equity funds.

Singularity Equity Fund I: Managed by V Ramanan, follows bottom-up stock selection, focusing on a concentrated portfolio of listed and pre-IPO opportunities. It utilizes a proprietary PE DNA model with a cross-over focus, ensuring depth and rigor in investment decisions, and aims to deliver superior returns by integrating both macro themes and detailed company analysis.

  • March 2025: Revenue stood at Rs 26,965.86 Cr, a decrease compared to Rs 29,180.02 Cr in March 2024. Net profit significantly increased to Rs 3,974.62 Cr from Rs 265.58 Cr in the same period last year. EPS also saw a substantial rise, reaching Rs 32.98 compared to Rs 3.49.

  • September 2024: Revenue was Rs 22,608.07 Cr, accompanied by a net profit of Rs 1,890.98 Cr and an EPS of Rs 14.82.
  • June 2024: The company posted revenue of Rs 25,472.40 Cr, a net profit of Rs 1,648.20 Cr, and an EPS of Rs 12.30.

Annual Performance:
  • March 2025: Revenue was Rs 97,894.75 Cr, and net profit stood at Rs 7,497.26 Cr. The EPS was Rs 60.55, with a book value per share of Rs 435.92. The Return on Equity (ROE) was 14.10%, and the debt-to-equity ratio was 1.52.
  • March 2024: Revenue totaled Rs 96,420.98 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 3,293.40 Cr and an EPS of Rs 27.24.
  • March 2023: The company reported revenue of Rs 136,977.76 Cr, a net profit of Rs 2,208.94 Cr, and an EPS of Rs 21.78.
  • March 2022: Revenue was Rs 69,420.18 Cr, net profit was Rs 475.37 Cr, and EPS was Rs 7.06.
  • March 2021: Adani Enterprises posted revenue of Rs 39,537.13 Cr, with a net profit of Rs 746.32 Cr and an EPS of Rs 8.39.

Key Financial Metrics


Analyzing various financial ratios provides further insight into the company's performance:
  • Basic EPS (Rs.): Increased from Rs 8.39 in March 2021 to Rs 60.55 in March 2025.
  • Book Value per Share (Rs.): Rose from Rs 171.94 in March 2021 to Rs 435.92 in March 2025.
  • Return on Equity (%): Increased from 5.37% in March 2021 to 14.10% in March 2025.
  • Debt to Equity (x): Fluctuated over the years, standing at 1.52 in March 2025.

Cash Flow Analysis


The consolidated cash flow statement reveals the following trends:
  • Cash Flow from Operating Activities: Stood at Rs 4,513 Cr in March 2025, compared to Rs 4,093 Cr in March 2021.
  • Cash Flow from Investing Activities: Showed an outflow of -Rs 26,258 Cr in March 2025.
  • Cash Flow from Financing Activities: Recorded an inflow of Rs 21,947 Cr in March 2025.

Adani Enterprises' financial data indicates strong growth in net profit and EPS over the past year. According to Moneycontrol analysis as of 2025-05-12, the sentiment for the stock is very bullish.

With the stock currently trading at Rs 2,549.10, Adani Enterprises has demonstrated a positive trend in today's session, contributing to its position among the NIFTY 50 STOCKS.

Disclaimer This is an AI-assisted live blog with updates sourced from multiple news outlets and agencies Disclaimer

Hindustan Aeronautics experienced a notable surge during today's trading session, with its shares climbing by 3.83% to reach Rs 5051.00. The increase was marked by high trading volumes, signaling significant investor interest.

The increase in both volume and price has drawn attention from market participants and retail investors. Hindustan Aeronautics is a constituent of the NIFTY NEXT 50 and NIFTY 100 indices, highlighting its importance in the Indian stock market.

Financial Performance Overview


The positive investor sentiment aligns with Hindustan Aeronautics' recent financial performance.

Quarterly Performance (FY 2024-25):


  • March 2025 Quarter: Revenue stood at Rs 13,699.85 Cr, compared to Rs 6,957.31 Cr in December 2024.
  • Net Profit: Rs 3,959.47 Cr for the March quarter, up from Rs 1,434.36 Cr in the previous quarter.
  • EPS (Earnings Per Share): Rs 59.46 in March, compared to Rs 21.53 in December.

Annual Performance:


Over the past five years, Hindustan Aeronautics has demonstrated consistent growth in revenue and profitability:
  • Revenue: Increased from Rs 22,754.54 Cr in 2021 to Rs 30,980.95 Cr in 2025.
  • Net Profit: Rose from Rs 3,234.25 Cr to Rs 8,323.40 Cr during the same period.
  • EPS: Increased from Rs 96.87 to Rs 125.07.
  • Book Value Per Share (BVPS): Improved from Rs 461.02 in 2021 to Rs 523.07 in 2025.
  • ROE: Remained strong, with a value of 23.91 in 2025.
  • Debt to Equity: Maintained at 0.00, reflecting a strong financial position.

Cash Flow Analysis


Hindustan Aeronautics' cash flow from operating activities has generally increased over the years:
  • Cash Flow from Operating Activities: Rs 13,643 Cr in March 2025, compared to Rs 15,262 Cr in March 2021.
  • Cash Flow from Investing Activities: -Rs 10,771 Cr in March 2025, compared to -Rs 1,270 Cr in March 2021.
  • Net Cash Flow: Rs 293 Cr in March 2025, compared to Rs 6,866 Cr in March 2021.

Balance Sheet Highlights


The company's balance sheet shows consistent growth in assets and liabilities:
  • Total Assets: Increased from Rs 51,945 Cr in March 2021 to Rs 106,266 Cr in March 2025.
  • Total Liabilities: Increased from Rs 51,945 Cr in March 2021 to Rs 106,266 Cr in March 2025.

Key Financial Ratios


Key financial ratios provide insights into Hindustan Aeronautics' performance:
  • Gross Profit Margin: 39.28% in March 2025, compared to 25.08% in March 2021.
  • Operating Margin: 34.95% in March 2025, compared to 19.90% in March 2021.
  • Net Profit Margin: 26.86% in March 2025, compared to 14.21% in March 2021.
  • Return on Equity: 23.91% in March 2025, compared to 21.01% in March 2021.
  • Debt to Equity Ratio: Maintained at 0.00.

Valuation


Valuation ratios show how the market values Hindustan Aeronautics:
  • P/E Ratio: 33.40 in March 2025.
  • P/B Ratio: 7.98 in March 2025.
  • EV/EBITDA: 19.81 in March 2025.
  • P/S Ratio: 9.01 in March 2025.

According to Moneycontrol analysis as of today, the sentiment for Hindustan Aeronautics is very bullish. With a strong financial foundation and positive market sentiment, Hindustan Aeronautics demonstrates its position in the aerospace and defense sector.
